title: cardamine schinziana f. lasiocarpa
intro: Cardamine schinziana f. lasiocarpa, also known as the alpine bittercress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) native to the Alps. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as a natural diuretic. The plant is said to possess health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving menstrual cramps, and aiding in the treatment of urinary tract infections. Alpine bittercress can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused into oil for topical use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
